zh.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley II是Hugh Calverly和Agnes Anne Hawberk的兒子，於1387年7月21日出生在英國爾卡爾維勒。他於大約1429年與Maud Huberk在英國英格蘭爾切斯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1433年在英國爾享年46歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley II是Hugh Calverly和Agnes Anne Hawberk的儿子，于1387年7月21日出生在英国爾卡爾維勒。他于大约1429年与Maud Huberk在英国英格兰爾切斯特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1433年在英国爾享年46岁，埋葬在英国英格兰爾。

nameMeanings.name: Calveley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Calverley
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): habitational name from Calverley in Yorkshire, named with Old English calfra (genitive plural of calf ‘calf’) + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. Alternatively, in some instances the name may be a variant of the habitational name Calveley, from a place so called in Cheshire (with the same meaning as above), or from Calverleigh in Devon, meaning ‘the clearing of the bare wood’ (Old English calu ‘bare, bald’ + wudu ‘wood’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’).

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Japanese: written 井伊 ‘well’ and ‘that’, the name one of Japan's great daimyō families, originally based in Tōtōmi (now part of Shizuoka prefecture), later in Kōzuke (now Gunma prefecture) and Ōmi (now Shiga prefecture). Other variants of the name substitute either character with similar sounding characters such as 飯 ‘cooked rice’, 居 ‘residence’, and so forth. It was formerly Romanized as Iyi. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number II (meaning ‘the second’, i.e. ‘the younger’ of the two bearers of the name) is apparently also counted as a surname Ii.

History: Ii Naomasa (1561–1602) was awarded lands in Kōzuke and Ōmi for his service in the civil wars that won the shōgunate (military dictatorship) for Tokugawa Ieyasu. His family built the beautiful castle of Hikone, which still stands. His descendant Ii Naosuke (1815–1860) ruled Japan for two years as Tairō (Great Elder) and played a major part in signing treaties that opened Japan to foreign trade at the end of the Tokugawa Shōgunate. He was assassinated by adherents of the anti-foreign party.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

en.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley II was born on 21 July 1387, in Calveley, Cheshire, England as the son of Hugh Calverly and Agnes Anne Hawberk. He married Maud Huberk about 1429, in Chester, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1433, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 46, and was buried in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley II est né le 21 juillet 1387, à Calveley, Cheshire, Angleterre, fils de Hugh Calverly et Agnes Anne Hawberk. Il a épousé Maud Huberk environ 1429, à Chester, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1433, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 46 ans, et a été enterré à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley II wurde am 21. Juli 1387 in Calveley, Cheshire, England, als Sohn von Hugh Calverly und Agnes Anne Hawberk geboren. Er hat um 1429 in Chester, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Maud Huberk geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1433 im Alter von 46 Jahren in Cheshire, England und wurde in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley II è nato il il 21 luglio 1387 nel Calveley, Cheshire, England come figlio di Hugh Calverly e Agnes Anne Hawberk. Lui sposato Maud Huberk circa 1429, nel Chester, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1433, nel Cheshire, England, all'età di 46 anni, e fu sepolto a Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley IIは1387年7月21日にイギリスチェシュアイアカルベリーでHugh CalverlyとAgnes Anne Hawberkの息子として生まれた。約1429年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアチェスターでMaud Huberkと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1433年にイギリスチェシュアイアで46歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスチェシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley II(은)는 1387년 7월 21일 영국 체실르 캘벨리에서 Hugh Calverly(와)과 Agnes Anne Hawberk의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1429년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 체스터에서 Maud Huberk(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1433년 영국 체실르에서 46세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 체실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley II nasceu em 21 de julho de 1387, em Calveley, Cheshire, Inglaterra filho de Hugh Calverly e Agnes Anne Hawberk. Ele casou-se com Maud Huberk aproximadamente 1429, em Chester, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1433, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 46 anos, e foi sepultado em Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley II родился 21 июля 1387 г. в местности калвели, чешир, Англия. Его родителей звали Hugh Calverly и Agnes Anne Hawberk. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud Huberk около 1429 г. в местности честир, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1433 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 46 лет и был похоронен в местности чешир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Hugh Calveley II nació el 21 de julio de 1387, en Calveley, Cheshire, Inglaterra como hijo de Hugh Calverly y Agnes Anne Hawberk.
